title: Migratory Bird Flight Path Educator
description: An interactive 3D educational simulation where students learn about bird migration by guiding a stork along a globe-spanning flight path while Kira acts as an animated teaching companion. Users must correctly sequence waypoints, adjust altitude for weather zones, and answer ecology quiz prompts to complete the migration journey.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load both shared_assets/stork.glb and shared_assets/kira.glb. Build a stylized flat terrain plane with a subtle grid texture representing a map. Place 6 numbered waypoint markers (glowing spheres with labels like 'Europe', 'Sahara', 'Nile Delta', 'Savanna', 'Coast', 'Destination') along a curved migration path using a CatmullRomCurve3. Position the stork model at the start waypoint and Kira model in the bottom-right corner of the scene on a small pedestal as a teaching guide. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 15, 20) looking at the terrain center, with OrbitControls limited to prevent going below the ground plane. Add a DirectionalLight with shadows enabled, an AmbientLight, and a HemisphereLight for natural outdoor feel. Interaction steps: (1) Click the stork to begin the lesson, triggering Kira to play an idle animation and an HTML overlay text box to show welcome text. (2-6) For each of the 5 intermediate waypoints, the user must click the correct next waypoint in sequence. Validate that waypoints are clicked in order; show red flash for wrong picks. When correct, animate the stork along the curve segment using TWEEN or requestAnimationFrame lerp, adjusting the stork's Y position with a sine wave to simulate flapping altitude. (7) At waypoints 2 and 4, display a multiple-choice quiz overlay about migration facts with 3 options. Validate the answer and show green or red feedback on Kira's pedestal via emissive material color change. (8) After reaching the final waypoint, trigger a celebration: rotate Kira with a spin animation, orbit the stork in a circle, and display a completion banner with the user's score out of the quiz questions. Track state with a stepIndex variable and validate each interaction before advancing. Add an HTML overlay panel for instructions, quiz questions, and score display styled with semi-transparent dark background and white text.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentWaypointIndex: number,         // index of the waypoint the stork is currently at or heading toward (0-5)
  totalWaypointsReached: number,         // count of waypoints successfully reached by the stork
  storkAltitude: number,         // current Y-position altitude of the stork model in scene units
  quizQuestionsAnswered: number,         // count of ecology quiz questions correctly answered
  kiraAnimationActive: boolean,         // whether Kira teaching companion is currently playing an animation
  lessonStarted: boolean,         // whether the user has clicked the stork to begin the lesson
  wrongWaypointClicks: number,         // count of incorrect waypoint clicks made by user
  storkFlightProgress: number,         // normalized progress (0.0-1.0) along current flight segment between waypoints
  weatherZoneActive: boolean,         // whether stork is currently in a weather zone requiring altitude adjustment
  finalDestinationReached: boolean,         // whether stork has completed the full migration path
}
